name: Chocolate-Cinnamon Dessert Nachos
recipeIngredient: 1 cup heavy cream or whipping cream
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350°F.
2. In a small bowl, mix together 2 tablespoons granulated sugar and cinnamon.
3. Melt 4 tablespoons butter.
4. Brush melted butter on both sides of the tortillas.
5. Sprinkle the cinnamon-sugar mixture on one side of each tortilla.
6. Stack the tortillas on top of each other, sugared sides up, and cut into 10 to 12 wedges.
7. Place the tortilla wedges in a single layer on buttered baking sheets.
8. Bake for 12 to 14 minutes or until the tortilla wedges are crisp and golden brown.
9. Meanwhile, in a medium saucepan, combine cream, remaining 1/2 cup granulated sugar, and brown sugar.
10. Heat over medium heat, stirring often, until boiling.
11. Boil for 5 minutes, stirring occasionally, until slightly thickened.
12. Remove from heat; stir in vanilla extract.
13. Transfer half of the mixture to a bowl.
14. Add melted chocolate to the remaining mixture and stir.
15. To the remaining mixture, stir in 1 tablespoon butter.
16. Let both sauces cool to lukewarm.
17. Assemble nachos by scattering tortilla pieces in a single layer over a large serving platter.
18. Drizzle some chocolate sauce and caramel sauce over the tortillas and sprinkle about 3 tablespoons nuts on top.
19. Repeat layers until all ingredients are used.
20. Serve immediately.
